headline: Agriculture Sector Shrinks to 6.8% of GDP — Lowest Ever
context: Agriculture's share of Malaysian GDP fell to 6.8% in 2025, the lowest in the nation's history, even as 1.8 million workers — 12% of the workforce — remain employed in the sector at average wages below the poverty threshold.

cards.big: Agriculture shrinks to 6.8% of GDP but still employs 12% of workers — the definition of a low-productivity trap
cards.sub: Average agricultural worker produces RM38,000 in GDP versus RM112,000 in manufacturing — a 3x productivity gap

cards.big: Farm income per worker rose only 8% in real terms over 10 years — versus 34% growth in services sector
cards.sub: Without palm oil, agricultural GDP contribution is only 2.4% — food farming alone is economically marginal

cards.big: 62% of farmers are over 55 years old with average monthly income of RM1,400 — below poverty line
cards.sub: Youth agricultural participation dropped to 4.2% of farming workforce — lowest generational engagement on record

cards.big: Agriculture Ministry's RM8.2 billion budget allocates 58% to subsidies and only 14% to modernisation and technology
cards.sub: Subsidy-first approach maintains low-productivity farming rather than enabling transition to high-value agriculture

cards.big: Subsidising poverty-level farming keeps 1.8 million workers trapped — the humane policy is investment in productivity, not handouts
Should Malaysia redirect 50% of agricultural subsidies into mechanisation grants and smart farming technology adoption?
